preview_text:
The model rates Argentina as a heavy favorite, assigning them a 76% chance of victory. Austria's probability of a win stands at just 4%, reflecting a substantial gap in the forecast outcome space.

The win-draw-loss distribution suggests Argentina enters as clear favorites, yet a draw holds meaningful probability at 20%. The 76-4 split in outright wins indicates the model expects Argentina to control much of the match, though the 20% draw likelihood signals that a stalemate remains a plausible outcome rather than a remote possibility.

The model and sportsbook consensus diverge notably. The market assigns Austria 17% to win compared to the model's 4% — a gap of 13 percentage points — and places Argentina at 59% rather than 76%. Polymarket pricing aligns more closely with the sportsbook view than with the model on Argentina's win probability. The dashboard does not adjudicate which source is more accurate; the divergence may reflect differing risk appetites, market liquidity, or information sets across venues.
